description:
## Goal

Add a Crystal spec for `RefreshLoop::SemaphorePool`
(`src/fetcher/semaphore_pool.cr`) that documents the class
contract and exercises `reset_for_testing` for the first time.

## Why now

- The class is the single owner of the concurrency primitives
  that used to live as module-level `@@` state in
  `refresh_loop.cr` (refactor ticket `quickhea-az6.1`).
- It has a `reset_for_testing` hook specifically for spec use.
  No spec has been written to exercise it yet.
- A spec gives the supervisor and the per-feed fetcher a
  reference for what the pool promises (limit, repair,
  concurrent acquire/release).
- The `quickhea-alz` P0 memory-growth investigation may need to
  instrument the pool; a spec gives that work a safe place to
  assert invariants.

## Coverage

- `#initialize` — default limit, custom limit, the channel is
  pre-filled to capacity
- `#acquire` / `#release` — single-threaded counter behavior
- `#acquire` blocks when at capacity (with timeout in a fiber)
- `#health_status` — reports available/expected correctly
- `#repair` — returns 0 at capacity, refills missing slots
- `#reset_for_testing` — refills to full capacity (this is the
  spec's raison d'être; document the expected behavior)
- `#release` is safe after a `Channel::ClosedError` (defensive
  rescue)

## Acceptance

- `spec/semaphore_pool_spec.cr` exists and follows the existing
  spec conventions (see `spec/minhash_spec.cr` for style).
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec spec/semaphore_pool_spec.cr`
  passes (all examples green).
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec` (full suite) still
  passes 180 examples.
- `nix develop . --command ameba spec/semaphore_pool_spec.cr`
  is clean.
title: Add spec/semaphore_pool_spec.cr for SemaphorePool
